issues.title: Define echo/reply packet type for delay measurement
issues.author: ihlar
issues.body:
A third POEM packet type for endpoint-to-endpoint delay measurement. One endpoint sends an echo, the peer replies. On-path observers time the echo and reply to measure RTT and split it into upstream/downstream components.

Payload bits could be used to encode reply delay, possibly using some logarithmic encoding. This would make measurements more precise than the spin bit. 
Report packets can be added for delay as well. 
Sending frequency could be left to endpoints or be defined by the protocol. It could range from once per RTT to occasional pings.
